hook: AI gave me a 5-paragraph essay when I needed a yes or a no.
caption:
AI gave me a 5-paragraph essay when I needed a yes or a no.

You asked: "Should I take the meeting?"

Generic AI said: "There are several factors to consider when evaluating whether a meeting aligns with your professional goals. First, assess the potential value of the connection..."

You closed the tab.

---

Here's the thing — the AI wasn't wrong. It just doesn't know that you're a bullet-point, bottom-line-first, don't-explain-the-journey-just-tell-me-the-destination kind of thinker.

It doesn't know you've already done the analysis in your head. You don't need it walked back to you in paragraph form. You need a decision, a reason, and maybe a flag if something's off.

That's not a prompt problem. That's a *profile* problem.

When your AI knows how your brain processes information — your pace, your tolerance for nuance, whether you want options or a single recommendation — every response lands differently.

Same question. Completely different answer.

"Skip it. Low signal, no clear ask from their side. If they follow up with an agenda, revisit."

That's 22 words. That's what you actually needed.

BootFile builds the profile so you stop rewording prompts and start getting responses that match how you actually think.
